text.title: Solo Support SLA Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding solo-support-sla-template. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: SLA policy MUST define exactly 3 tiers (Community, Paid, Enterprise) with explicit response-time bands. Tier creep (4+ tiers solo) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Three tiers maps to billing reality (free / paid / enterprise); more tiers create maintenance burden the solo operator cannot sustain.

rule.statement: SLA MUST publish an explicit business-hours boundary (timezone + days). 24/7 promises by a solo operator are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit 24/7 from a solo operator burns the operator and erodes long-term reliability.

rule.statement: Policy MUST ship ≥4 canned replies: acknowledgment, deflection-to-docs, escalation, after-hours. Free-form replies for routine cases are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Canned replies preserve calendar; tone consistency improves perceived reliability.

rule.statement: Policy MUST define an escalation trigger (e.g. paying customer with 0 reply in 48h auto-escalates to operator's phone). Implicit escalation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without explicit trigger, breaches go unnoticed until churn signals.

rule.statement: Policy MUST flag tickets with churn signals (cancellation, downgrade, 'thinking of switching') for same-day reply regardless of tier.
rule.rationale: Churn signals often bury in routine tickets; same-day reply is the highest-leverage save.
